title: malus oleosa
intro: Malus oleosa, also known as the wild olive tree or the bitter apricot, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Malus oleosa include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Malus oleosa include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and infections.
